rationale: Circulating supply grew approximately 8M RENDER over the trailing 12 months (~388M to ~396M), implying ~2.1% annualized inflation under the Burn-and-Mint Equilibrium model where new tokens are minted only when GPU render jobs are paid. With ~140M tokens remaining before the hard cap of 536,870,912 is reached and no mechanism to accelerate emissions beyond organic network demand, the effective inflation rate is structurally declining over time.
caveats: Precise trailing-12-month gross mint volume from Solana on-chain data requires aggregation not available on free-tier explorers; the 0.4% figure is approximated from CoinGecko circulating supply snapshots.
BME model means emissions are demand-driven, not schedule-driven; actual rate could be higher or lower depending on network render job volume.
Messari granular emission data requires paid subscription: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER
